---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "concatenate_spatialdata"
namespace: "Dataflow"
description: "Concatenate multiple SpatialData objects into one SpatialData output"
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `concatenate_spatialdata`  
Namespace: `dataflow`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/dataflow/concatenate_spatialdata)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/dataflow/concatenate_spatialdata)

:::



## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/dataflow/concatenate_spatialdata/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/dataflow/concatenate_spatialdata).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/dataflow/concatenate_spatialdata/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name       |Description                                                             |Attributes                                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--inputs` |Paths to Zarr stores containing the SpatialData objects to concatenate. |List of `file`, required, multiple_sep: `","` |


### Outputs

|Name       |Description                                                           |Attributes                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output` |The output Zarr store containing the concatenated SpatialData object. |`file`, required, default: `"output.zarr"` |


### SpatialData options

Options for how to concatenate the SpatialData objects, passed to
`spatialdata.concatenate`.



|Name            |Description                                                                                                                                |Attributes |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--attrs_merge` |The method to use for merging elements in `attrs` when concatenating the SpatialData objects. See `anndata_merge` for options and details. |`string`   |


### AnnData options

Options for how to concatenate the AnnData objects when concatenating the
main tables of the SpatialData objects using `anndata.concat`.



|Name                  |Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |Attributes                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--anndata_join`      |The type of join to perform on the AnnData objects when concatenating the main tables of the SpatialData objects:  - "inner": Only variables (genes) that are present in all objects are   kept. - "outer": All variables (genes) that are present in any object are   kept. Sparse arrays are padded with zeros, other objects are filled   with missing values.  See `anndata.concat` for details.                                                                       |`string`, default: `"inner"`  |
|`--anndata_merge`     |The method to use for merging elements that are not aligned with `obs` when concatenating the main tables of the SpatialData objects:  - None: No elements are kept. - "same": Only elements that are the same across all objects are kept. - "unique": Elements for which there is only one possible value are   kept. - "first": The first element at each position is kept. - "only": Elements that are only in one object are kept.  See `anndata.concat` for details. |`string`                      |
|`--anndata_uns_merge` |The method to use for merging elements in `uns` when concatenating the main tables of the SpatialData objects. See `anndata_merge` for options and details. Merging is applied recursively.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |`string`                      |
|`--anndata_label`     |The name of the column in `obs` of the concatenated main table to store batch label information. If not provided, no batch label column is added.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |`string`, example: `"sample"` |
|`--anndata_pairwise`  |Whether pairwise elements (`obsp`/`varp`) are concatenated.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |`boolean`, default: `FALSE`   |

## Authors


  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (maintainer)

  * Luke Zappia [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/lazappi) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7744-8565) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/lazappi) (author)
